# WI-QUIC-CRT-0044 - QUIC client-side replacement peer Initial reset work item

## Summary

Add the narrow client-side pre-transcript reset boundary for replacement peer Initial packets that prove a new server attempt, while keeping same-attempt retransmissions, Retry ownership, and broader multi-attempt behavior out of scope.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0146

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0042

## Planned Changes

- Track the first accepted peer Initial source connection ID together with the first offset-zero Initial CRYPTO prefix while the client runtime is still establishing.
- When a later peer Initial changes both markers before the peer transcript completes, reset the current peer-handshake attempt state and reapply the replacement Initial through the normal Initial path.
- Clear buffered Initial ingress CRYPTO, transcript offsets, and derived Handshake/open material from the earlier peer attempt so the replacement attempt can rederive cleanly.
- Keep same-crypto source-connection-ID drift on the current attempt instead of resetting it.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for positive replacement-attempt reset and negative same-crypto no-reset behavior, and preserve captured replay evidence for the multiconnect replacement-server path.

## Out of Scope

- general multi-attempt handshake orchestration
- server-role replacement-attempt handling
- Retry ownership or token lifecycle changes beyond REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122
- public API widening
- transfer support widening
- retry support widening
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the pre-transcript reset boundary

## Verification Plan

Run the focused requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122 and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0146 together with the captured multiconnect replay tests, rerun the helper-backed client-role `quic-go` `multiconnect` interop reproduction, then render and validate the SpecTrace markdown and JSON artifacts.

## Completion Notes

Implemented the narrow client-side replacement peer Initial reset boundary, retained same-attempt retransmissions when the Initial CRYPTO prefix stays the same, added focused requirement-home coverage, and preserved captured replay evidence from the multiconnect replacement-server path.
